gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found injected-service memory reader mismatches and explicit-writer bypasses; the workflow now follows the active materializer rule, copies generated/preloaded memory bytes into service readers when safe, rejects incompatible memory generation up front, and GPT-5.5 approved d49c543.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_workflow.py tests/test_kvpack.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q -> 103 passed
what_changed: Added write_kvpack_bytes(...) for in-memory kvpack shard serialization with manifest ChunkRef metadata.
Taught DocumentKVWorkflow memory-capable workflows to generate memory:/mem: shards into the active materializer memory reader, including injected-service readers and preloaded memory_blobs.
Rejected memory shard generation when the active materializer cannot load memory refs, preventing accidental local files and late prepare-time failures.
why: Cachet needs Memory to be a first-class generated shard target for hot ephemeral document KV caches, not only a reader for manually preloaded blobs.
